_notes: Most widely used herbicide in AU. Dozens of brands with aerial labels.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (drones = aircraft per APVMA). Many glyphosate labels include aerial directions.
aerialRate: 2–6 L/ha (rate varies by target weed and growth stage)
bestTiming: Apply to actively growing weeds with adequate leaf area. Avoid moisture stress.
adjuvantNotes: Most formulations self-surfactant. Do NOT add extra surfactant unless label directs.

_notes: Pasture broadleaf — thistles, dock, capeweed, fireweed, Paterson's curse
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (drones = aircraft per APVMA). CAUTION: High drift risk — Coarse+ droplets mandatory, observe buffer zones.
aerialRate: 1.5–3 L/ha (rate varies by weed species and growth stage)
bestTiming: Apply to actively growing rosette-stage weeds. Spring application before bolting is critical for thistles.
adjuvantNotes: Most amine formulations self-surfactant. Do not add oil-based adjuvants — increases drift.

_notes: Phenoxy broadleaf for pasture. Complementary to 2,4-D.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (drones = aircraft per APVMA). CAUTION: Drift risk — use Coarse+ droplets.
bestTiming: Apply to actively growing rosette-stage broadleaf weeds. Avoid application during drought stress.
adjuvantNotes: Most formulations self-surfactant. Do not add oil-based adjuvants.

_notes: Broadleaf selective in grass. Good on woody seedlings and hard-to-kill broadleafs.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial where label includes aerial directions (check specific product label).
bestTiming: Apply when weeds are actively growing. Best on young growth — less effective on mature woody plants.
adjuvantNotes: Wetting agent at label rate (e.g. BS1000). Oil-based adjuvants may improve uptake on waxy leaves.

_notes: Broadleaf selective in pasture. Extreme drift/volatility risk — strong warnings needed.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial where label includes aerial directions. EXTREME CAUTION: Highly volatile — mandatory buffer zones, temperature restrictions, and Coarse+ droplets.
bestTiming: Apply when weeds are actively growing. DO NOT apply when temp >28°C due to volatility. Avoid inversions.
adjuvantNotes: Per label only. Do NOT add extra surfactants or oil — increases volatility and drift.

_notes: Non-selective residual. Total vegetation control for rights-of-way, woody weeds.
weed: Rubber vine / woody weeds / total vegetation
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial where label includes aerial directions. Non-selective with soil residual — do NOT use near desirable vegetation.
bestTiming: Apply to actively growing woody weeds. Soil residual prevents regrowth for 12+ months.
adjuvantNotes: Wetting agent at label rate. Surfactant improves foliar uptake on woody species.

_notes: Parthenium is a WoNS in QLD — common drone target.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (drones = aircraft per APVMA). Parthenium is a Weed of National Significance (WoNS).
bestTiming: Apply to rosette-stage parthenium before bolting. Once flowering, efficacy drops significantly.
adjuvantNotes: Self-surfactant amine formulations. Do not add oils.

_notes: Parthenium alternative — metsulfuron provides longer residual.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (drones = aircraft per APVMA).
bestTiming: Apply at rosette to early bolt stage. Residual activity suppresses germinating seedlings for 3–6 months.
adjuvantNotes: BS1000 wetting agent at label rate.

_notes: QLD WoNS. Aggressive woody vine — major threat in tropical/sub-tropical.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial where label includes aerial directions. Rubber vine is a WoNS.
bestTiming: Apply during active growth (wet season). Full leaf coverage critical for systemic uptake.
adjuvantNotes: Pulse Penetrant at 200 mL/100 L — essential for waxy vine leaves.

_notes: Rubber vine with imazapyr for long residual.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial. Non-selective with long soil residual — keep away from desirable vegetation.
bestTiming: Wet season active growth. Soil residual prevents regrowth for 12+ months.
adjuvantNotes: Wetting agent at label rate.

_notes: QLD WoNS — tropical woody shrub.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial where label includes aerial directions. Bellyache bush is a WoNS.
bestTiming: Apply during wet season active growth before seed set.
adjuvantNotes: Pulse Penetrant at 200 mL/100 L.

_notes: Common pasture weed — NSW/VIC/SA. Purple bugloss.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (drones = aircraft per APVMA). Paterson's curse is a WoNS.
bestTiming: Apply at rosette stage before bolting (typically late autumn–winter). Once flowering, efficacy drops.
adjuvantNotes: Self-surfactant amine. Do not add oils.

_notes: Paterson's curse alternative with residual.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial.
bestTiming: Apply at rosette to early bolt stage. Provides residual suppression of germinating seedlings.
adjuvantNotes: BS1000 wetting agent at label rate.

_notes: Giant Parramatta grass — NSW invasive grass. Same chemical as GRT.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (drones = aircraft per APVMA).
bestTiming: Active growth phase with adequate soil moisture.
adjuvantNotes: NO surfactant — label prohibits. Do not tank mix with surfactants.

_notes: Coolatai grass — NSW invasive. Flupropanate is primary chemical option.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (drones = aircraft per APVMA).
bestTiming: Active growth with good soil moisture. Spring–summer.
adjuvantNotes: NO surfactant — label prohibits.

_notes: Scotch/Spear/Variegated thistle — extremely common across SE Aus.
weed: Scotch thistle / spear thistle / variegated thistle
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (drones = aircraft per APVMA). Use Coarse+ droplets — drift sensitive.
bestTiming: Rosette stage — BEFORE bolting. Once stem elongation starts, efficacy drops drastically.
adjuvantNotes: Self-surfactant amine. Do not add oils or penetrants.

_notes: Thistles alternative — clopyralid is highly effective on Asteraceae.
weed: Scotch thistle / spear thistle / variegated thistle
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial where label includes aerial directions. Highly selective — safe in grass.
bestTiming: Apply rosette to early bolt stage. Provides excellent residual on Asteraceae family weeds.
adjuvantNotes: Wetting agent at label rate.

_notes: Cat's claw creeper — SE QLD/northern NSW. Environmental weed.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (label may specify helicopter — drones included under APVMA; use caution).
bestTiming: Apply during active growth when vine canopy is visible. Repeat treatments needed for underground tubers.
adjuvantNotes: Pulse Penetrant at 200 mL/100 L — essential for vine leaf penetration.

_notes: Groundsel bush — NSW coast. Bitou bush relative.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (label may specify helicopter — drones included under APVMA).
bestTiming: Apply during active growth before flowering. Spring–early summer.
adjuvantNotes: BS1000 wetting agent at label rate.

_notes: Bitou bush — NSW coastal environmental weed.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (label may specify helicopter — drones included under APVMA).
bestTiming: Apply during active growth. Late spring–summer. Follow-up for seedlings from soil bank.
adjuvantNotes: BS1000 wetting agent at label rate.

_notes: Privet — common environmental weed across eastern AU.
droneStatusNote: Permitted via aerial (label may specify helicopter — drones included under APVMA; use caution).
aerialRate: Per aerial label rates for each product
bestTiming: Apply during active growth with full leaf cover. Spring–summer.
adjuvantNotes: Pulse Penetrant at 200 mL/100 L.
